topics.title: Creepy Twins, Religious Fanaticism and a Loaded Gun Collide in Tense Chamber Drama Godhead — Fantasia
topics.angle: Mark H. Rapaport's new film 'Godhead' is premiering at the Fantasia festival, noted for its tense atmosphere.

sources.headline: Creepy Twins, Religious Fanaticism and a Loaded Gun Collide in Tense Chamber Drama Godhead — Fantasia
sources.summary: Mark H. Rapaport s new film “Godhead” is a master-class in ratcheting up tension. Shot in black-and-white with camerawork that makes the audience feel like a voyeur uncovering perverse secrets, the film blends faith, sex, lies and discomfort into an unknowable dance between its leads. The premise begins simply: A rural Catholic priest (Al Warren) is [ ]
